decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.28°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to improve lateral heat dissipation away from the chip. I will apply a conservative 1.5x increase to the spreader material conductivity, staying well within the allowed 2.0x step ratio and upper bound of 500.0 W/(m*K).

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the thermal gradient under the chip and lowering the peak temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature below the 85.0°C constraint.
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader layer.
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate or boundary conditions rather than the spreader itself.
High conductivity materials may be cost-prohibitive in real-world applications, though this is a simulation study.
